name: LLM Gateway (theopenco)
tagline: One OpenAI-compatible API to route, manage, and track LLM requests across providers
seoTitle: LLM Gateway - Open-Source Multi-Provider LLM Proxy
metaDescription: LLM Gateway is an open-source API gateway that routes requests to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and more through one OpenAI-compatible API with cost and usage tracking.
overview: LLM Gateway is an open-source API gateway that sits between your applications and multiple LLM providers. Instead of integrating each provider separately, you send requests to one endpoint that speaks the OpenAI API format and forwards them to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Vertex AI, and others.

It is aimed at teams that call several LLM providers and want a single place to manage API keys, watch token usage and costs, and compare model performance. Because the interface matches the OpenAI format, existing OpenAI client code can point at the gateway with minimal changes.

As an LLM gateway/proxy, it adds a routing and observability layer to your stack. You can run the hosted version at llmgateway.io or self-host the unified Docker image for full control over your data and configuration.
features: OpenAI-compatible API so existing OpenAI clients work with little change
Routes requests across multiple providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Vertex AI
Central management of provider API keys in one place
Usage analytics for requests, tokens, response times, and costs
Performance monitoring to compare models on speed and cost-effectiveness
Self-host with the unified Docker image or use the hosted service

gettingStarted.intro: You can use the hosted service at llmgateway.io or self-host with Docker. The quickest path is to get an API key and call the gateway's OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint.

steps.heading: Call the hosted API
steps.body: Get an API key from llmgateway.io, then send a chat completion request to the gateway. The endpoint follows the OpenAI format, so you pass a model and a messages array.

steps.heading: Self-host with Docker
steps.body: Generate the required secrets and run the unified container script. Use Docker-managed named volumes for Postgres and Redis rather than bind-mounting a host directory.
steps.code:
export LLM_GATEWAY_SECRET="$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n')"
export GATEWAY_API_KEY_HASH_SECRET="$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n')"
./scripts/run-unified-container.sh

steps.heading: Or run a one-off container
steps.body: If you prefer a single docker run, create named volumes first and pass the auth secrets as environment variables.
steps.code:
docker volume create llmgateway_postgres
docker volume create llmgateway_redis

docker run -d \
  --name llmgateway \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 3002:3002 -p 3003:3003 -p 3005:3005 \
  -p 3006:3006 -p 4001:4001 -p 4002:4002 \
  -v llmgateway_postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data \
  -v llmgateway_redis:/var/lib/redis \
  -e AUTH_SECRET="$LLM_GATEWAY_SECRET" \
  -e GATEWAY_API_KEY_HASH_SECRET="$GATEWAY_API_KEY_HASH_SECRET" \
  ghcr.io/theopenco/llmgateway-unified:latest

useCases: Route an app across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google through one endpoint without writing per-provider integrations
Track token usage and spend across all LLM calls in a single dashboard
Manage and rotate provider API keys centrally instead of spreading them across services
Compare models on latency and cost to decide which to use in production
